title: Using lambda expressions in loops should be avoided in Blazor markup section
description: Using lambda expressions in loops should be avoided in Blazor markup sectionAnalyze your codeintentionality-efficientmaintainabilityCode SmellblazorWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoIn Blazor, usinglambda expressionsasevent handlerswhen the UI elements are rendered in a loop can lead to negative user experiences and performance issues. This is particularly noticeable when rendering a large number of elements.The reason behind this is that Blazor rebuilds all lambda expressions within the loop every time the UI elements are rendered.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CI©2025SonarSource Sàrl. All rights reserved.Privacy Policy|Cookie Policy|Terms o
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.

checks.message: Avoid using lambda expressions as event handlers in loops in Blazor - they are rebuilt on every render causing performance issues

logic.detection_conditions: Lambda expression used as event handler inside a loop
logic.triggering_conditions: AND: (1) Inside foreach or for loop AND (2) Lambda expression assigned to handler (() => or => method)
logic.reasoning: In Blazor, lambda expressions used as event handlers in loops are rebuilt on every render cycle. This causes unnecessary memory allocations and can lead to performance issues, especially with large lists. Using child components with callbacks or pre-bound methods avoids this overhead.

should_trigger: foreach (var item in items) { button.OnClick = () => HandleClick(item); }
for (int i = 0; i < items.Count; i++) { elem.Click = () => Process(items[i]); }
